title: Homeopathy method intake (structured clinical data)
description: Method profiles + global fields. Kingdom and miasm addons merged after clinical blocks (separate JSON). Potency, Hering, HOM-CASE, HCCR below.

sub_fields.label: Medication profile (nomenclature, trade names, potency & scale)
sub_fields.placeholder: Per HOM-CASE: remedies/constituents, potencies, manufacturer if known…

sub_fields.label: Dosage / repetition pattern
sub_fields.placeholder: How given: frequency, in water, plussing, etc.

sub_fields.label: Objective evidence (labs, imaging, exam)
sub_fields.placeholder: HOM-CASE: external measurements or clinician-observed findings where applicable…

sub_fields.label: Homeopathic aggravation
options: Possible — mild, transient per guideline definition
sub_fields.helper: Expected mild transient worsening after dose that resolves with overall improvement (HPUS clinical data / HOM-CASE).

sub_fields.label: Aggravation details
sub_fields.placeholder: Timing, duration, relationship to dose, subsequent improvement…

sub_fields.label: Characteristic symptom 1 (triad)
sub_fields.placeholder: First striking, individualizing symptom…

sub_fields.label: Prognostic factors / LR-style reasoning (optional)
sub_fields.placeholder: Symptoms as prognostic factors; Bayes / likelihood-ratio style notes if documented…

sub_fields.label: MONARCH total score (if calculated)
sub_fields.placeholder: e.g. 8

sub_fields.label: Domain scores or narrative
sub_fields.placeholder: Modified Naranjo for homeopathy: temporal relation, dechallenge, direction of cure, etc.

sub_fields.label: Symptom similarity (patient vs remedy)
options: High — many characteristic / PQRS features match → higher potency bias
Low — few reliable matches → lower potency bias
sub_fields.helper: High similarity → higher susceptibility → higher potency tendency; low similarity → lower potency (per classic teaching).

sub_fields.label: Age (reactivity cue)
options: Child / young adult — often higher reactivity
Elderly — often lower reactivity (senile change cue)

sub_fields.label: Constitution / temperament
options: Nervous / sanguine / choleric — quicker reaction; high-potency bias in teaching
Phlegmatic / slower reaction — low-potency bias in teaching

sub_fields.label: Habits & environment
options: Sedentary / intellectual work — often higher susceptibility in texts
Chemical / tobacco exposure — often lower-potency cue

sub_fields.label: Pathological / tissue change
options: Advanced structural / organ change — lower-potency cue in texts

sub_fields.label: Seat of disease (organ involvement)
options: Vital organ prominently involved — low-potency cue in teaching

sub_fields.label: Nature & intensity (acute vs chronic)
options: Acute — recent; memory fresh; high similarity may favour higher potency
Chronic — long duration; similarity often weaker → moderate / lower potency bias

sub_fields.label: Stage / duration (chronicity)
options: Prolonged chronic — low-potency / gentle repetition cue in teaching

sub_fields.label: Previous allopathic / suppressive treatment
options: Substantial prior — some teachers note curative response may need higher potency

sub_fields.label: Form & quantity (globules, liquid, etc.)
sub_fields.placeholder: Galenic form, number of drops / pills, in water, etc. — minimum effective stimulus.

sub_fields.label: Repetition principle (Organon 6th §245–251 spirit)
options: Do not repeat while the previous dose is still acting beneficially
Repeat only when prior dose clearly exhausted / picture plateaus
Acute / emergency — shorter intervals per case
Chronic — single dose may act a long time; minimal repetition
sub_fields.helper: Classic rule: not too seldom, not too often — wait for the remedy to complete its useful action before repeating.

sub_fields.label: If repeating in acute course: interval
sub_fields.helper: High potency often single dose; in acute/emergency, repeats at short intervals may be used per judgment.

sub_fields.label: Rule 1: Not repeating / changing while patient is clearly improving
Rule 2: Not changing while course follows Hering’s law (within→out, above→down, reverse order)
Rule 3: Not changing remedy when discharge or eruption follows the dose (observe)
If rules don’t apply — explain
sub_fields.placeholder: Brief justification when the classic hold-rules are overridden…

sub_fields.label: Potency & repetition — clinical notes
sub_fields.placeholder: Tie together similarity, seat, pathology, acute/chronic, minimum dose, and your repetition plan…

sub_fields.label: Summary rationale category (cross-check with Potency selection block above)
sub_fields.helper: Shortcut label; detail belongs in Potency selection & repetition and notes.

globalFields.label: Hering's law markers (tick at follow-up if observed)

sub_fields.label: Symptoms resolve in reverse order of appearance
Brief return of old symptoms then improvement

globalFields.label: Susceptibility (1–10)
globalFields.helper: Relates to posology; align with potency rationale above.

globalFields.label: Differential diagnosis / remedies ruled out
globalFields.placeholder: Remedies considered and excluded, with rationale…

globalFields.label: Red-line / success markers at follow-up
globalFields.placeholder: Which outcomes must move for this prescription to be on track?

globalFields.label: HCCR / repository submission (aligns with MoHFW Ayush HCCR guidance)

sub_fields.label: Prerequisites noted
sub_fields.helper: HCCR expects a registered portal user and awareness of published terms; consent rules below apply before identifiable data is shared.

sub_fields.label: Consent pathway (identifiable living patient vs exceptions)
options: Written patient consent — HCCR form (EN/HI) obtained & on file
Guardian consent — patient lacks decision-making capacity
Doctor’s declaration only — retrospective / no fresh patient consent possible
Verbal consent only — declaration route per HCCR
Not applicable — fully de-identified narrative (confirm locally)
sub_fields.helper: HCCR: publication of identifiable living patient data needs explicit consent; upload signed consent where required. Doctor declaration applies only under portal rules for defined situations.

sub_fields.label: Consent / declaration documentation
sub_fields.placeholder: Where the signed patient consent or doctor’s declaration is stored (e.g. clinic file ID, upload filename on HCCR, date). HCCR requires upload where applicable.

sub_fields.label: Case-report keywords (2–5)
sub_fields.placeholder: CARE/HOM-CASE-style keywords for indexing…

sub_fields.label: De-identified / limited dataset sharing (if no identifiers submitted)
Target repository & submission status
sub_fields.placeholder: e.g. HCCR draft submitted; journal; other registry…

profiles.title: 8-Box Synergy framework (cross-verification)
profiles.helper: Remedy confidence grows when the same pattern appears across multiple ‘boxes’ of the case.

fields.label: Other boxes / cross-check notes (free text)
fields.placeholder: Brief notes for boxes 1–3, 5–6, or cross-cutting themes not captured above…

profiles.title: Sensation / vital level (depth of experience)
profiles.helper: Science of the case improves when the level of experience is explicit (name → fact → … → energy).

fields.label: Deepest level reached in case-taking
fields.helper: Select the deepest level the patient reached during the interview.

fields.label: Non-human experience (the ‘it’)
fields.placeholder: Hand gestures, source language, abstract descriptions of core sensation / energy…

fields.label: Kingdom / family notes (optional)
fields.placeholder: Plant / mineral / animal mapping, row or group, if used…

profiles.title: Boenninghausen / Boger (complete symptom, analogy)
profiles.helper: Strongest for physically-led cases; build each complete symptom clearly.

sub_fields.label: Location & extension
sub_fields.placeholder: Where and how it spreads…

sub_fields.label: Sensation (PQRS)
sub_fields.placeholder: Quality of pain / sensation…

sub_fields.label: Modality (better / worse)
sub_fields.placeholder: Aggravations and ameliorations…

sub_fields.label: Concomitant (associated symptoms)
sub_fields.placeholder: Apparently unrelated symptoms that move with the chief…

fields.label: Further complete symptoms (optional)
fields.placeholder: Second or third complete symptoms if needed for repertorisation…

profiles.title: Kentian / mental totality (hierarchy)
profiles.helper: Top-down: will and intellect before particulars when this method leads.

sub_fields.label: The will (loves, hates, fears)
sub_fields.placeholder: Aversions, desires, fears, striking will-related symptoms…

sub_fields.label: Intellect (memory, concentration)
sub_fields.placeholder: Cognitive pattern, mistakes, time sense…

sub_fields.label: Morals & disposition
sub_fields.placeholder: Ethical stance, guilt, honour, sensitivity to injustice…

fields.label: Generals & particulars (supporting)
fields.placeholder: Physical generals and locals that confirm or qualify the mental picture…

fields.label: Physical generals & PQRS
fields.placeholder: Thermal, thirst, appetite, sleep, energy, rare/strange symptoms…

fields.label: Modalities
fields.placeholder: Better / worse from time, weather, motion, food…

profiles.title: Protocol / Banerji / formula
profiles.helper: Named lines, potency rules, and when to advance or change.

fields.label: Protocol or remedy lines
fields.placeholder: Named protocol, remedy sequence, potencies and spacing…

fields.label: Indication in this patient
fields.placeholder: Why this protocol fits this presentation…

fields.label: Follow-up / change rules
fields.placeholder: When to repeat, change potency, add next line…

profiles.title: General method notes
profiles.helper: When no keyword profile matches; still use scientific add-ons below.

fields.label: How you are applying the selected method
fields.placeholder: Brief focus for this prescription round…

fields.label: Case notes relevant to remedy choice
fields.placeholder: Structured or narrative data you want captured…
